title: Embedded Rebar Corrosion Rate Monitor
description: Electrochemical corrosion monitor for steel rebar embedded in concrete. Performs periodic LPR polarisation scans using a potentiostat circuit, computes corrosion rate via the Stern-Geary equation, classifies active/passive state, transmits results over Modbus RTU, and raises fault alarms on sensor disconnection or active corrosion threshold breach.
objective: Measure rebar corrosion rate every 15 min; classify active (> 0.1 µA/cm²) vs passive; transmit Modbus RTU frame; alarm on active threshold; log to serial.
constraints: Arduino Pro Mini 3.3 V; potentiostat on PCB; Modbus RTU RS-485; IP68 enclosure; powered from 3.7 V LiPo; BOM < $45.
supporting_fact_titles: Linear Polarisation Resistance Corrosion Rate Measurement for Steel
Concrete Hydration Exotherm and Thermal Gradient Limits
element_usage_notes: Potentiostat applies ±20 mV polarisation; INA128 shunt reads current µA for LPR calculation
DAC sets OCP-relative bias voltage for each polarisation step in LPR scan
Transmits Modbus holding register frame with corrosion rate and status every 15 min
Entire electronics assembly potted inside enclosure; electrode leads exit via M12 connectors

element_interactions.description: DAC sets polarisation voltage applied to rebar working electrode
Computed CR and active/passive flag packed into Modbus holding registers
Enclosure shields potentiostat electronics from concrete moisture and alkalinity
Open-circuit potential measured before scan; DAC bias referenced to OCP
